LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT QINGYE WANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON OCTOBER 23, 2023, IS GRANTED. BASED ON THE COMPANION MOTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA HAS JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. THERE IS NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT REACHED OUT TO CALIFORNIA AND PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HIMSELF OF ITS BENEFITS. THE DEFAULT IS THEREFORE VOID. ORDER IS SIGNED. . JUDGE: RICHARD B. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Caixing Ma, Esq., ARDENT LAW GROUP, P.C. (949) 299-0188 mma@ardentlawgroup.com (949) 299-0188
Attorneys for Plaintiff GigaCloud Technology
Christopher G. Boscia, Esq., BOSCIA LEGAL, PC, chris@boscialegal.com (408) 753-6224 Attorneys for Defendant QINGYE WANG

Not contested. Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT QINGYE WANG , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Defendant's motion to set aside the default entered on October 23, 2023, is granted. Based on the companion motion, plaintiff fails to show that California has jurisdiction over defendant. There is no competent evidence showing that defendant reached out to California and purposefully availed himself of its benefits. The default is therefore void.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Caixing Ma, Esq., ARDENT LAW GROUP, P.C. (949) 299-0188 mma@ardentlawgroup.com (949) 299-0188
Attorneys for Plaintiff GigaCloud Technology
Christopher G. Boscia, Esq., BOSCIA LEGAL, PC, chris@boscialegal.com (408) 753-6224 Attorneys for Defendant QINGYE WANG

Not contested. Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT QINGYE WANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Defendant's motion to quash is granted. A party may bring a motion to quash with a motion to vacate under CCP 473 and 473.5. (CCP 418.10(d).) Plaintiff has the burden to demonstrate jurisdiction. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440). Plaintiff relies on the declaration of Mr. Lau to demonstrate that defendant Wang had minimum contacts with California.

order is singed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
